description: Declaring an improvement or regression from one run, mistaking environmental noise for a real performance change.
body: Failure mode: baseline and candidate are each run once and the difference is reported as a result, but the gap is within run-to-run variance from scheduling, caches, thermal state, or background load. Detection signals: the delta vanishes or reverses on repeat; no variance reported; the difference is smaller than typical noise. Do not conclude from a single sample. Safer fallback: take multiple samples after warmup, compare distributions against the noise floor with a confidence statement, and only report a change that exceeds variance and repeats.
